SEARCH
Package de.jvstvshd.necrify.api.message

Interface MessageProvider

All Known Implementing Classes:
ResourceBundleMessageProvider
public interface MessageProvider
An interface that is responsible for providing messages to the user. The messages are displayed internationalized to the end user, so the implementation should be able to provide messages in different languages or a default language.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type

    Description

    autoPrefixed()
    Returns whether this message provider automatically prefixes messages.

  • Method Details

    • provide

      @NotNull @NotNull net.kyori.adventure.text.Component provide(@NotNull @NotNull String key, @Nullable @Nullable Locale locale, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component... args)
      Provides a message to the user with the given key in the given locale. If the locale is null, the system's default Locale is used.
      Parameters:
      key - an unique key identifying the message to provide.
      locale - the locale in whose language the message should be provided.
      args - additional components that fill in placeholders in the message.
      Returns:
      the translated and formatted message.

    • provide

      @NotNull @NotNull net.kyori.adventure.text.Component provide(@NotNull @NotNull String key, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component... args)
      Provides a TranslatableComponent containing the message specified by the given key. This does not need a locale as it is determined by the server when sending the [TranslatableComponent] to the client.
      Parameters:
      key - an unique key identifying the message to provide.
      args - additional components that fill in placeholders in the message.
      Returns:
      the formatted message ready to be translated to the user.

    • internalError

      @NotNull @NotNull net.kyori.adventure.text.Component internalError(@Nullable @Nullable Locale locale)
      Provides a message to the user describing that an internal error occurred while executing a command. The message is prefixed depending on the value of autoPrefixed().
      Parameters:
      locale - the locale in whose language the message should be provided. If locale is null, the system's default Locale is used.
      Returns:
      the translated message stating the occurrence of an internal error.

    • internalError

      @NotNull @NotNull net.kyori.adventure.text.Component internalError()
      Provides a message to the user describing that an internal error occurred while executing a command. The message is prefixed depending on the value of autoPrefixed(). It is translated when sent to the user as a TranslatableComponent.
      Returns:
      an translatable component stating the occurrence of an internal error.

    • prefix

      @NotNull @NotNull net.kyori.adventure.text.Component prefix()
      Provides this system's prefix to display in front of messages.
      Returns:
      the prefix component

    • provideString

      @NotNull default @NotNull String provideString(@NotNull @NotNull String key, @Nullable @Nullable Locale locale, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component... args)
      Provides a message to the user with the given key in the default locale. This yields the same result content-wise as calling provide(String, Locale, Component...), but as String instead of a Component.
      Parameters:
      key - an unique key identifying the message to provide.
      locale - the locale in whose language the message should be provided. If locale is null, the system's default Locale
      args - additional components that fill in placeholders in the message.
      Returns:
      the translated and formatted message as a string.

    • prefixed

      @NotNull default @NotNull net.kyori.adventure.text.Component prefixed(net.kyori.adventure.text.Component... args)
      Prefixes the provided components into one component.
      Parameters:
      args - the components to prefix.
      Returns:
      one prefixed component.

    • defaultLocale

      @NotNull default @NotNull Locale defaultLocale()
      Provides the default locale of the system. This is used when no locale is provided in the methods or the translation does not exist in the provided locale. Per default, this is Locale.ENGLISH.
      Returns:
      the default locale of the system.

    • autoPrefixed

      boolean autoPrefixed()
      Returns whether this message provider automatically prefixes messages. If this is true, the message provider will automatically prefix messages with the result of prefix(). This behaviour can be bypassed by using an unprefixedProvider(), though this will only be supported on autoPrefixed variants.
      Returns:
      whether this message provider automatically prefixes messages.

    • autoPrefixed

      default void autoPrefixed(boolean autoPrefixed)
      Sets whether this message provider should automatically prefix messages.
      Parameters:
      autoPrefixed - true if messages should be automatically prefixed, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this message provider does not support changing the auto-prefixing behavior (default behaviour)

    • unprefixedProvider

      default MessageProvider unprefixedProvider()
      Returns a new message provider that does not prefix messages.
      Returns:
      a new message provider that does not prefix messages.
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this message provider does not support changing the auto-prefixing behavior (default behaviour) or does not have a fixed auto-prefix setting
